methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $350M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Boston Metal, a Series C climate and energy technology company founded by Tadeu Carneiro, is evaluated against a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector comparable companies: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). The broader comp universe includes eight private-stage peers and one public company (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $350M+ total capital raised as a proxy base valuation, applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier typical of frontier-tech progression, yielding $595M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the three selected comparables, which exhibit a $700M–$2.0B range with a median of $1.0B. Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $506M, median of $798M, and high estimate of $1.1B.

The valuation range of $506M–$1.1B reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate-tech valuations. The median estimate of $798M sits between the stage-progression result and peer median, weighted equally. Key sensitivities include technology maturity relative to comparables, path to commercialization, and capital efficiency—variables not fully captured in backward-looking fundraising data. The 2.2x spread between low and high reflects the discrete peer range and methodology divergence.
